MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCE:
Ariel M. Gershon, Esq., for plaintiff and moving party: Ajila Hart
David S. Semel, Esq., for defendant and opposing party: Mathhew Cohen

HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff Ajila Hart's motion to compel attendance at deposition is denied as moot.
If further documents are produced pursuant to the order relating to plaintiff's motion to compel supplemental responses and documents, the Court would entertain a motion to re-open the depositions of defendants. All requests for monetary sanctions are denied. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN.

Notice Of Pltfs' Motion To Commpel Defts To Answer Questions And Produce Documents At Deposition, To Appear At Deposition, To Cease Obstructing Witness Depositions And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar May-08-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
David S. Semel, Esq., for defendant and moving party: Matthew Cohen
Steven McDonald, Esq., for plaintiff and opposing party: Ajila Hart

HEARING HELD and tentative ruling is adopted. Plaintiff Ajila Hart's motion to compel answers to deposition questions is Granted in part and denied in part.
The motion is granted as to the questions set forth in the separate statement of matters in dispute. As to the categories of requested documents, the motion is granted as to #37. The motion is denied as to #40, 41, 47, 57, and 58.

The motion for an order directing defendant to not obstruct certain depositions is moot in light of the ruling regarding defendant's motion to quash. The motion for an order compelling defendant Richard Frenzel to appear for deposition is denied at this time. The parties are ordered to meet and confer as to a mutually convenient date.
The request for monetary sanctions is denied.
Deposition to be completed by June 29, 2007. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN.
